title: [SECURITY/ARCH] gVisor breaks the shared-netns pod: agent can't reach sidecar on 127.0.0.1 under runsc
description:
LOCAL-VERIFIED (this host, runsc release-20260525.0). A real goose+sidecar spawn was driven under both runtimes via standalone spawnlet + spawnctl 'what is the secret word?':
- runc: goose reached the sidecar (127.0.0.1:8080), full ACP round-trip, real LLM reply ('The secret word is QUOKKA-4417'). WORKS.
- runsc: goose BOOTED + ACP-initialized + accepted the prompt (so goose IS gVisor-compatible — no syscall/ENOSYS issues), but failed with 'Network error: Could not connect to 127.0.0.1:8080'. The SIDECAR IS UNREACHABLE.
ROOT CAUSE: our pod is two Docker containers sharing a netns (agent started with --network=container:<sidecar>, reaches the sidecar's inference proxy on loopback). Under per-container gVisor, EACH container is its own Sentry with its OWN user-space netstack — Docker's container:<id> netns-sharing does NOT bridge the two Sentries' loopbacks, so the agent's 127.0.0.1 is not the sidecar's. The gVisor research flagged this: k8s puts a WHOLE POD in ONE runsc sandbox; Docker 'docker run' per-container does not.
=> gVisor adoption (sp-7k8) CANNOT use the current Docker two-container shared-netns pod. Options to design: (a) run the pod as ONE runsc sandbox (containerd CRI pod / k8s RuntimeClass — whole pod = one Sentry, shared loopback, the documented model); (b) collapse the sidecar into the agent container (single container = agent + inference-proxy process, one sandbox, loopback works); (c) separate netns + agent reaches sidecar via bridge IP:8080 (weakens isolation, cross-container exposure, and conflicts with the RFC1918 egress drop — messy). DESIGN-FIRST. This is a HARD prerequisite for Phase-2 gVisor on cloud; verified that goose, hardening (cap-drop), preflight, and the host egress floor all work under runsc — only the inter-container loopback breaks.
notes: DECISION (user 2026-06-01): for RUNSC use a one-sandbox pod via containerd CRI (RunPodSandbox handler=runsc + 2 containers: sidecar+agent, separate mount/pid ns sharing the pod netns -> loopback works, KEY STAYS ISOLATED in the sidecar). For RUNC keep the current Docker two-container shared-netns approach unchanged. FEASIBILITY CONFIRMED on this host: containerd 1.7.24 CRI plugin enabled+ok, /run/containerd/containerd.sock live, containerd-shim-runsc-v1 present. STILL NEEDED: (1) containerd config — add a [plugins...cri.containerd.runtimes.runsc] handler (runtime_type io.containerd.runsc.v1) + restart; (2) CNI for pod-sandbox networking (cri plugin needs a CNI bridge plugin/config). EPIC SCOPE (large): node gains a CRI pod backend (k8s.io/cri-api gRPC client to the containerd sock) selected when CONTAINER_RUNTIME=runsc; manager.Create branches runc(Docker two-container) vs runsc(CRI pod); sub-systems: CRI RunPodSandbox/CreateContainer/StartContainer x2, CRI ImageService pull (containerd's k8s.io image namespace is SEPARATE from Docker's — images must be pulled there), stdio Attach to the agent container over CRI streaming for the ACP bridge, egress floor on the POD IP but the CNI bridge is not docker0 so DOCKER-USER may not be in-path -> floor likely needs a raw-FORWARD/custom host chain for CRI pods, RemovePodSandbox teardown, runsc preflight under CRI. Cleanest seam: a runtime.Pod abstraction both backends implement (Docker=2 containers shared netns; CRI=pod). DESIGN-FIRST.

title: [SECURITY] CP must authenticate nodes + verify cloud-class — today NODE_CLASS/NODE_OWNER are self-asserted on an UNAUTHENTICATED stream (cloud-spoofing)
description:
CRITICAL TRUST GAP. The node<->CP Attach stream has NO auth (cmd/cp/main.go:103 'node side: no auth'); a node self-reports node_class + node_owner in its Register message (sp-2as/sp-t5p). So ANY node that can reach the CP can claim NODE_CLASS=cloud (and any node_owner) and be treated as a trusted, Spawnery-operated cloud node. Combined with class/owner-based routing (sp-t5p) and any future cross-user/secret data flow (vended storage tokens, other users' spawns, audit data), a malicious self-hosted node could SPOOF a cloud spawnlet and receive other users' secret data. The cloud/self-hosted trust split (sp-rpa floor enforcement, sp-t5p routing) currently RESTS ON unauthenticated self-assertion — only safe today because the demo runs solely Spawnery-operated nodes on a closed CP.

NOTE: there is NO node keypair / secret-passing crypto in the codebase yet (only crypto/tls transport + crypto/rand for ids). The 'spawnlets generate pubkeys for secret passing' is E0/E4 DESIGN, deferred in the demo cut — not built. So this must be designed, not just wired.

PROPOSED (user 2026-06-01): cloud spawnlets present a node identity cert whose pubkey is signed by Spawnery's ROOT CA; CP PINS that root and verifies the chain on Attach before honoring class=cloud or routing any cross-user/secret data. Self-hosted nodes are unauthenticated/lower-trust and may only run their OWN owner's spawns (aligns with sp-t5p: unverified->author's-own-node; a self-hosted node should be bound to a single authenticated owner, and CP must verify node_owner == the connecting identity, not trust the self-reported value). Likely: per-node keypair generated on the node; CSR/enrollment signed by Spawnery CA for cloud nodes; mTLS or signed-Register-token on the Attach stream; CP rejects class=cloud without a valid Spawnery-CA-chained cert; CP derives node_owner from the authenticated identity for self-hosted, not from the self-reported field. Interacts with E4 identity/secrets (sp-7h6) and the sp-gtm CP-key-vending MITM finding. DESIGN-FIRST. Until landed: document that NODE_CLASS/NODE_OWNER are trusted only in a closed all-Spawnery-operated deployment.

title: [bug] egress floor is in-pod-netns iptables — gVisor netstack only partially supports iptables; move floor to host veth
description: HOST-VERIFICATION RESEARCH (gvisor.md §4): our sp-rpa egress floor applies iptables INSIDE the container netns via nsenter. Under runc that's the real kernel netns (verified working). Under gVisor (runsc, netstack), the pod's netns is the Sentry's user-space TCP/IP stack which only PARTIALLY supports iptables — our DROP rules may silently behave differently / not enforce. Since we plan to adopt gVisor for the cloud tier, the floor must move OUT of the pod netns to the HOST: enforce on the host veth ingress hook / DOCKER-USER chain / CNI, where the real Linux netfilter sees gVisor packets exiting the veth normally. Affects firewall.NsenterApplier placement. Pre-req for gVisor adoption; also more robust under runc. Re-verify metadata+RFC1918 drop from the host side.
notes: EMPIRICALLY PROVEN on this host (runsc release-20260525.0). IN-NETNS floor (current NsenterApplier) under runsc = COMPLETE NO-OP: host-netns iptables DROP counters all 0 pkts, RFC1918 10.0.0.1 REACHABLE (vs blocked + 3-pkt counters under runc). gVisor netstack handles TCP/IP in user space + emits raw frames on the veth, so host-kernel netfilter in the container netns never sees workload traffic. => flipping on runsc TODAY would silently make the egress floor a no-op (metadata/RFC1918/exfil all open). HARD gVisor pre-req, P0. FIX VALIDATED: host DOCKER-USER rules matched by the pod's bridge source IP enforce under BOTH runtimes — under runsc, -s <containerIP> -d 169.254.0.0/16/-d RFC1918 DROP matched 3 pkts each, public reachable, dns :53 ACCEPT present. IMPL: get the SIDECAR container's bridge IP (docker inspect NetworkSettings.IPAddress — the netns owner; agent shares it via container:netns so no own IP); insert ordered DOCKER-USER rules (-s ip): udp/tcp 53 ACCEPT + EgressAllowCIDRs, then 169.254/16 + RFC1918 DROPs (insert reversed so final order is accepts-before-drops, or use a per-spawn child chain). NEW LIFECYCLE: DOCKER-USER persists across containers (unlike the auto-cleaned netns) — manager.Stop MUST remove this spawn's rules; track the IP per spawn. loopback (agent<->sidecar 127.0.0.1) is never forwarded so the host floor doesn't touch it. 172.16/12 drop is fine (blocks cross-container/internal; egress dest is external/public, allowed).
close_reason: Done+host-verified, merged b9f5342: egress floor moved from in-netns nsenter (PROVEN no-op under gVisor/runsc) to host DOCKER-USER chain matched by pod bridge IP (firewall.HostFloorApplier + Rules(ip); ContainerIP). Apply-on-create fail-closed, REMOVE-on-stop (FloorIP tracked). Shipped egress_e2e PASSES as root (metadata+RFC1918 dropped, public reachable) under runc AND validated equivalent under runsc. Unblocks gVisor adoption.

title: DEMO: automated App-review scanner is a committed demo build item
description: Open third-party publishing was chosen for the demo, so the E8 §5 LLM-as-judge App-review scanner (prompt-injection / data-exfiltration / jailbreak / scope-sanity over manifest+persona+skills) is NO LONGER deferred -- it gates every open publish and is the thing that makes open publishing safe. Real ML/eval build item. Run it OFF the DeepSeek inference GPU (sp-iui). Tune against curated red-team prompts + the seed apps. Affects E8 §5, E5 open-registry gate.
notes: REFRAMED via trust tiers (user decision): apps carry a per-version trust tier — unverified (published, not scanned) / scanned (passed this automated scanner) / reviewed (human). Runtime restrictions + user-facing warnings SCALE with tier; the enforced sandbox floor (egress + isolation + per-app consent) applies to ALL tiers. So the scanner is the 'scanned' tier mechanism, NOT a perfect single gate — a scanner miss is contained by the floor + the tighter unverified-tier sandbox + informed-consent disclosure. This de-risks the scanner's load-bearingness. Tier drops to 'scanned' on new version; permission escalation drops 'reviewed'. Seed apps ship 'reviewed'. Spec: E5 §5, E8 §5, scope §4.

title: DEMO-ROAST: threat-model inversion — user-steered agent IS the untrusted vector
description: The demo deferred egress enforcement (sp-ba5) and chose PLAIN CONTAINERS (no gVisor/microVM), both justified by 'no arbitrary creator code; Spawnery-vetted runtime only'. FALSE for the demo: the spawn runs a coding-style agent with shell/file/network tools, steered turn-by-turn by an untrusted free-tier stranger. The USER is the untrusted-code path. Consequences: open SSRF/egress-proxy/metadata-cred-theft by design (no egress allowlist), and container-escape/compute-abuse surface (plain containers + shell-capable agent). The security machinery cut for B+Y was load-bearing for B+Y too. FIX: treat the demo's spawn as hostile; pull egress floor (D2) + isolation hardening + resource limits (D3) + restricted toolset (D6) into the demo. Affects E1 isolation decision, E0/E8 egress, demo-mvp-scope.
notes: USER DECISION 2026-06-01: restricted agent toolset is POST-MVP/backlog. Strategy = allow-all tools by default; rely on REVIEW/AUDIT mechanisms (app-review scanner sp-5a9 + E8 audit) to detect abuse; escalate to VM/microVM isolation in the CLOUD if the platform takes off (not gVisor-per-spawn now). The OTHER sp-eha pieces ARE shipped: egress floor (sp-rpa), per-spawn cgroup limits + per-user quota + optional gVisor runtime knob (sp-ach). So sp-eha's hardening floor is substantially in place; only the toolset-restriction sub-item is deferred. Abuse-control for the demo now leans on sp-5a9 (scanner) + egress+limits floor + trust tiers + informed consent.

title: DEMO-ROAST: ship a hard egress allowlist floor in the demo (not post-MVP)
description: Egress enforcement was deferred as 'permissions/consent' (sp-ba5), but the per-spawn EGRESS FLOOR (a hard default, not per-app consent UI) is cheap and closes most network abuse. FIX: per-spawn network policy blocking everything except the localhost model sidecar + GitHub API/host + any app-declared domains; explicitly DROP 169.254.169.254 (cloud metadata) + RFC1918 internal ranges. Owner = E1 (pod netns). This is the single highest value/cost fix in the demo. Closes SSRF, egress-proxy, metadata-cred-theft, arbitrary-host exfil.
notes: CONFIRMED demo-required: egress floor is mandatory for the open marketplace.
close_reason: Egress block-floor implemented + merged 1435585: firewall pkg (Rules block-floor: drop 169.254/16 + RFC1918, allow lo + operator CIDRs, default-allow public; NsenterApplier), runtime.ContainerPID, manager.Create applies fail-closed BEFORE agent starts, node-class aware (cloud always enforces non-disableable; self-hosted honors EgressEnforce; default cloud). Hermetic tests (rule ordering, fail-closed, cloud-forces, self-hosted-opt-out) green; final review APPROVE. CAVEAT: real packet-drop UNVERIFIED in dev sandbox (no iptables/root) — build-tagged egress_e2e must run on node host (see sp-* follow-up).

title: ROAST: privacy/brand claims dishonest as written — reframe
description: Found by 2/4 (security #2/#3/#7, product F1-F5/F7). Multiple headline claims are weasel-worded for the cloud/free path: (a) 'E2E / CP never sees content' — channel terminates at the NODE, which on cloud IS Spawnery and audits content; (b) 'BYO key never reaches us' — on cloud nodes the node decrypts the plaintext BYO key + audits content; (c) 'your data stays yours' — CP holds GitHub-App signer + can mint write tokens to your repo silently; (d) 'one-click' — see ROAST-6. Reframe all copy: 'encrypted in transit to the Spawnery-operated node that runs your agent; on cloud, content is audited for abuse.' Reserve absolute privacy claims for self-host only. Affects system-design §1/§6/§7/§9.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: CP is total SPOF + relay bandwidth cliff
description: Found by ops #1/#2. The single central CP relays ALL non-LAN-direct session ciphertext (every token, every turn) AND mints tokens AND schedules. A CP restart/deploy/OOM drops every active conversation simultaneously and blocks new sessions = 100% cloud blast radius. No HA/drain/reconnect story. Relaying all conversation bytes = 2x egress through one box, saturates on bandwidth + connection count long before compute. FIX: split a stateless horizontally-scaled relay dataplane from control logic; session-resumption tokens (reconnect without re-auth); blue/green relay; prioritize LAN-direct/BYO-ingress off the post-MVP shelf; publish SLO + client reconnect behavior. Affects E0 §1/§10, E1.

title: ROAST: BYO-on-cloud audit has no inspection point — mechanical gap
description: Found by 2/4 (arch #1, security #3). Four docs assert 'BYO-on-cloud is still audited' but NO component sees BYO model traffic: the sidecar calls the provider directly (E2 §1), the node only terminates the transport channel + forwards ACP frames over loopback (not model prompts/responses), and the E8 classifier is scoped to 'Spawnery-operated inference' which BYO bypasses. FIX: make the SIDECAR the single audit/classifier point (it's the only component seeing all model traffic, managed + BYO; it's a Spawnery-controlled container). Reconcile every 'node is the audit point' phrasing to the sidecar. Affects E2 §1/§4, E8 §1/§2, E0 §9/§10, system §3.

title: ROAST: home-machine singleton + no DR/failover
description: Found by ops #3/#4. (a) One physical box runs the whole free tier + DeepSeek on one GPU; realistic ceiling ~5-20 concurrent generations; if it dies the free tier is hard-down (burst triggers on OVERLOAD from a live heartbeat, not on a DEAD node). (b) No backup/DR for CP state (spawn index, catalog, GitHub App signing JWT, OAuth refresh tokens, vault wraps, audit store) — DB loss = users can't find spawns, must re-auth all storage, vault ciphertext gone. FIX: active/standby or dead-node->cloud failover (not just overload->burst); decouple inference GPU pool from container CPU pool; CP DB backup/PITR/geo-replication; GitHub App key + OAuth secrets in KMS not the DB; document what's reconstructable from spawn.yml. Affects system §1, E1 §5, E0.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: CP key-vending enables trivial MITM — 'E2E' unverifiable
description: Found by 3/4 reviewers (security #1/#2, ops #10, product F3). The client receives the node static pubkey FROM the CP with no out-of-band verification (deferred post-MVP). A malicious/compromised CP substitutes its own key, runs key agreement as the client, decrypts BYO keys + full conversation, re-encrypts to the real node. Classic unauthenticated-DH key substitution. Worse: Spawnery is simultaneously CA + relay + key-directory, so even cert pinning doesn't stop the operator minting a node cert. The 'CP relays only opaque ciphertext / never sees content' claim is structurally false against the operator. FIX: vend full CA-signed cert chain + pin Spawnery CA root in the client bundle (not fetched at runtime); make out-of-band/TOFU node-key pinning MVP (esp. self-host); add key-transparency log. Affects E0 §10, E4 §6/§8, E6 §2.

title: Cross-component APIs & contracts
description: The shared seams every component depends on. Defines: spawneryapp.yml manifest schema; spawn.yml schema (data-repo source of truth, secrets excluded); container orchestration interface (start/exec/stop); ACP client<->agent orchestration contract; storage-adapter interface (materialize/persist); model-sidecar OpenAI-compatible API + central-gateway protocol + metering/audit hooks; CP index API; edge-activator session-token/auth protocol; permission/consent + egress-allowlist schema; catalog/registry index API. Design-first; all other epics consume these contracts. See design doc sections 2,3,5,6,7,8,9,10.
notes:
Design doc (v1, interview complete): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-26-spawnery-e0-contracts-design.md — see Appendix decision log (E0.1–E0.9).
v2 corrections: CP = single central managed instance (self-host = node agents attaching to it); rendezvous relay in CP; s2s=gRPC; Apps agent-agnostic (agent chosen at spawn, manifest declares compat); skills imported via agent's native process; storage detail -> E3; personalization + permissions -> post-MVP (TODO.md). Amended system-design §1/§4/§10.

title: Runtime & orchestration core
description: Control plane, node agent, container lifecycle (ephemeral, scale-to-zero), ACP orchestration layer, pluggable isolation-backend abstraction (concrete impls deferred), scheduler/placement (local-first, audit-flag set at placement). Design doc sections 3,4.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-spawnery-e1-runtime-core-design.md. Decisions: per-agent base image + mount definition (no per-(App,agent) build); cold-start every wake (warm pools post-MVP); placement = open app + owner node -> owner node else cloud; 2-D burst (CPU->offload containers, GPU->offload inference); idle/close/cap teardown, resume from /data; pod = base[agent+bridge+toolset]+sidecar; Docker/Podman backend first. Refined E0 §7/§3.

title: Spawn lifecycle slice 3: web multi-spawn UI (list, status dots, kebab actions, switch + replay)
description: App.tsx multi-spawn state (spawns Map + activeId, one live ws for activeId); ListSpawns hydrate + ~3s poll. Sidebar 'Spawns' list: status dot (green active/grey suspended/red errored-unreachable/amber transitional), name headline + app-id subline, kebab Rename/Suspend|Resume/Stop, double-click inline rename. Switch closes ws, restores items, opens ws if active, onHistory replaces items. api: listSpawns/renameSpawn/suspendSpawn/resumeSpawn/deleteSpawn. vitest + Playwright e2e (two secret-app instances, rename, suspend/resume, stop, switch+replay). See spec §Slice 3 / epic sp-k20.

On client attach, emit one out-of-band {method:spawn/history,params:{items}} frame. web/src/acp/client.ts gains onHistory + a route branch. Visual transcript only; lost on suspend. See spec §Slice 2 / epic sp-k20.

title: Per-backend ACP transport for rootless self-hosted (Docker attach + UDS) + empty-IP tolerance
description: GOAL: rootless self-hosted nodes on Mac AND Linux (incl. rootless Podman via Docker-compat socket); root only for cloud nodes is fine. PROBLEM: slice 1 (sp-7wa) unified BOTH backends on the UDS @spawnlet-acp + setns transport, which needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN (root) AND cannot cross the Docker Desktop macOS<->Linux-VM boundary (no setns on macOS; Unix sockets don't traverse virtiofs). So self-hosted-without-root and Mac Docker Desktop are both BROKEN today. FIX (per-backend transport): (1) add PodBackend.Attach(ctx,*PodHandle)(*AttachedStream,error); Docker impl = restore Docker stdio attach (rt.Attach(agentID), Mac+Linux, root-free, over the Docker API); CRI impl = AttachACP(netnsPath) (Linux+cloud+root, unchanged). (2) agent image entrypoint conditional on ACP_ADAPTER env: unset -> exec goose acp (PID1=goose, Docker-attachable); CRI backend sets ACP_ADAPTER=1 -> runs behind the adapter on the in-pod UDS. stubagent entrypoint -> direct (Docker-attachable). (3) route relay via Manager.Attach(ctx,sp)->m.pod.Attach; update server.go/ws.go/node/attach.go. (4) EMPTY-IP TOLERANCE (subsumes sp-mcb): DockerPodBackend.StartPod tolerates empty ContainerIP (rootless Podman slirp4netns/pasta has no bridge IP); Manager.Create fails-closed only if egressEnforced() && PodIP=='' . (5) docs: rootless self-hosted matrix (Docker / rootless Docker / rootless Podman DOCKER_HOST=podman.sock), update slice-1 CAP_SYS_ADMIN claims (now CRI-lane-only). RESULT: self-hosted Mac/Linux/Podman = no root; cloud CRI = root for floor only.
design: Per-backend transport. Reverses slice-1 unify-on-UDS (which broke non-root + Mac). Docker lane = Docker attach (no setns, no port, Mac via Docker API); CRI lane = UDS+setns (cloud, Linux, root). ACP_ADAPTER env toggles the agent entrypoint. Empty-IP tolerance needed for rootless Podman.
close_reason: DONE merged 80d1767. Per-backend ACP transport: Docker/runc lane = Docker stdio attach (rt.Attach, root-free, Mac+Linux+rootless Podman); CRI/runsc lane = UDS+setns (Linux+cloud). PodBackend.Attach + Manager.Attach routes the relay (Server.attach injection removed). Agent entrypoint toggles ACP_ADAPTER (Docker=goose PID1 attachable; CRI sets ACP_ADAPTER=1 -> adapter); stubagent direct. Empty pod IP tolerated (rootless Podman slirp4netns), Manager fail-closes only when egressEnforced()&&PodIP=='' (closes sp-mcb; CRI still requires IP). Image Go builders bumped to golang:1.26. Docs: CAP_SYS_ADMIN now CRI/cloud-only + rootless self-hosted matrix. RESULT: self-hosted Mac/Linux/rootless-Docker/rootless-Podman = NO root; cloud CRI = root for floor only. Final review: composes correctly, fail-closed invariant holds, no regression. Real Docker-attach-on-Mac + Podman ContainerAttach are host-verify.

title: [bug] egress floor blocks DNS when resolvers are RFC1918 (breaks inference)
description: HOST-VERIFIED finding (sp-rpa): on a host whose DNS resolvers are RFC1918 (e.g. 192.168.1.x home LAN, or cloud internal DNS), the egress block-floor's blanket RFC1918 DROP kills DNS resolution. Verified on the dev host: metadata+RFC1918 correctly blocked, public-by-IP (1.1.1.1) reachable, but DNS (resolvers 192.168.1.192/1.1) blocked -> curl 'could not resolve host'. DEMO-BREAKING: the sidecar resolves api.openrouter.ai, so inference breaks on every cloud spawn on such a host. FIX: firewall.Rules allow udp/tcp dport 53 ACCEPT before the RFC1918/metadata drops (standard egress-floor DNS carve-out). Residual: DNS-tunneling/internal-DNS-recon possible (acceptable for demo; audit layer covers content). Found by host-verification of the egress floor.
close_reason: Done+host-verified, merged 72256cf: added udp/tcp :53 ACCEPT before the RFC1918/metadata drops in firewall.Rules (resolvers are commonly RFC1918 -> blanket drop broke DNS -> broke sidecar->OpenRouter inference). egress_e2e now checks public egress by IP (1.1.1.1, DNS-independent) and PASSES on a privileged host; metadata+RFC1918 counter-proven dropped, DNS :53 counter-proven ACCEPTed. just test-egress recipe added. Unit test asserts DNS-before-drops.

title: Verify egress floor enforcement on a privileged node host (run egress_e2e)
description: sp-rpa shipped the egress block-floor but real packet-drop enforcement is UNVERIFIED in the dev sandbox (no iptables, non-root). Run the build-tagged test on a privileged host with Docker+iptables+root: 'go test -tags egress_e2e ./internal/spawnlet/firewall/ -run TestEgressFloorEnforced'. Asserts 169.254.169.254 + RFC1918 blocked, public (openrouter) reachable. Also confirm a real spawn pod (sidecar+agent shared netns) is firewalled end to end. Gates the demo's open-publishing safety claim.

title: Spawn lifecycle: active<->suspended, resume, idle-suspend, delete
description: Make the spawn lifecycle explicit and implement it: spawns are durable, resumable instances (not one-shot sessions). States starting/active/suspending/suspended/error/deleted. Operations: create, attach (auto-resume on open, single-session takeover), detach (container keeps running), suspend (explicit stop OR two-stage per-node idle timeout), resume (re-provision container w/ same config+backends), delete (preserve data backend by default). Suspend/resume is DATA-ONLY: full working tree incl uncommitted work persisted via a git WIP ref (refs/spawnery/suspend/<id>) that rides the existing GitHub-push / git-bundle-all paths; restored as unstaged on resume; fresh ACP session. CP/node restart reconciles in-flight/orphaned spawns to suspended. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-spawn-lifecycle-design.md (authoritative for the state machine; amends system-design 2/3). Touches CP index/DAO (status enum + fields), E0 contracts (ListSpawns/SuspendSpawn/ResumeSpawn/DeleteSpawn; Session auto-resumes; StopSpawn->suspend), node agent (clean-suspend/resume/idle timer), web client (list+status+controls).
design: Data-only resume (conversation continuity deferred). WIP-ref chosen over tar-archive (git-native, reuses both persist paths, bundle --all captures it free) and over stash-precise (less plumbing; dirty restored unstaged, fine for an agent). Idle = two-stage per-node: detached-short, attached-idle-long; relay frame in either direction = activity (node doesn't parse ACP). Single active container per spawn; single session w/ takeover. Delete preserves data by default ('your data is yours'); destroy is opt-in. HARD DEP: persistent storage backend (E3 managed) — resume is meaningless on Scratch. Honesty caveat: clean suspend lossless; hard crash lossy-to-last-checkpoint.
notes: SCOPE v3 (post 2nd roast): node-side owns — per-mount incremental persist (WIP branch spawnery-suspend/<id>/<gen>; GitHub pollution accepted, exclude from checks; blob via E3 INCREMENTAL bundle not --all; branch GC on every materialize) + restore; SERVER-SIDE backend write-fence by generation (rejects stale-gen writes from a partitioned/returned old container); node inventory reporting on Register/Heartbeat (RunningSpawn list); binds CP-sent mounts (not its own manifest re-parse, validate names vs manifest); two-stage idle timer (agent-stdio activity + event-driven detached timer); takeover fence; web UI incl. the 'unreachable' state + single Recreate/Wait control + scratch-reset & recovered notices. HARD PREDECESSORS: sp-pc4 + E3 persistent storage. Recovery is USER-DRIVEN: node-fail -> unreachable -> notify -> Recreate(or Wait); adopt if node returns first; on Recreate the old container is fenced+stopped. See specs.

title: Review authn/authz model — ensure every API is properly authorized
description:
Cross-cutting security review of the demo MVP's auth model: every API surface must authenticate the caller AND authorize the specific action/resource (no IDOR, no unauthenticated mutations). Audit each seam:

CLIENT-FACING (CP HTTP/OpenAPI):
- CP index API (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /spawns, POST /spawns/{id}/session): OWNERSHIP-scope every call — a user can only list/open/stop/delete THEIR OWN spawns. Guessing a spawnId must not grant access (IDOR).
- Catalog/registry API (GET /apps, POST /apps publish, ratings/flags/takedown): visibility scoping (private apps need entitlement); only the creator/owner can publish/modify their app; creator-capability gate.
- Session-token issuance: only the spawn owner gets a token for that spawn; token scoped to {spawnId, owner, node, exp}.

SERVICE-TO-SERVICE:
- CP<->node gRPC: mTLS + node identity bound to owner (per E4 §6); a node can't act for another's spawns; service-token validity/rotation.
- spawnlet ConnectRPC (CreateSpawn/Session/StopSpawn): TODAY THE SLICE HAS ZERO AUTH — anyone who can reach the spawnlet can create/attach/stop ANY spawn id. In production these come from the CP, so the spawnlet must authorize that requests originate from the legit CP (and Session must verify the session token binds the caller to the spawn). CLOSE THIS GAP when CP integration lands.
- Storage-token minting: CP-minted tokens scoped to exactly the user's repo (no over-broad scope); per E3 §3.

DATA/CONTROL:
- Per-app permissions + spawn-time consent + egress enforcement (sp-ba5) — the consent record must actually gate what the spawn can do.
- Audit-store break-glass access gated + logged (E8 §1); product-metrics (sp-yjw) content-free.
- Trust-tier capabilities enforced (unverified/scanned/reviewed gates what an app may request).

DELIVERABLE: a checklist of every endpoint x {authn? authz/ownership? IDOR-safe?}, gaps filed as issues, and the spawnlet-has-no-auth gap explicitly tracked to close at CP-integration time. Related: E0 contracts (sp-9zo), E4 identity (sp-7h6), E8 (sp-ydz); roast sp-gtm/sp-ba5/sp-gl2.

title: Concierge pre-build retention test (10-20 users, hand-held)
description: The cheapest test of the riskiest assumption (does a representative user retain to a personal-agent app), runnable NOW before the platform build. Recruit 10-20 Cohort-A/B target users (NOT viral-tweet crowd); for 2-3 weeks hand-run the top 1-2 candidate apps (Wiki+Research + Language Partner) with a HIGH-QUALITY model + manual glue (shared chat + manually maintained per-user state). Measure: unprompted week-2 return + qual (felt like mine? keep using? pay?). Decision rule: if they won't return when hand-held + excellent, the polished B+Y version won't save it -> don't build that app as flagship; pivot. ~0 engineering; removes all confounders (model/latency/onboarding). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-spawnery-demo-experiment-design.md §4.

title: Event cold-storage telemetry (hourly Parquet, content-free)
description: The CP emits SESSION-LIFECYCLE events to cold storage as HOURLY PARQUET files for offline metric analysis: who created which app session, when (spawn create/start/end, owner, app@sha, trust tier, storage type, node placement) -- METADATA ONLY, NO conversation/data content. PRINCIPLE (firm): product/engagement metrics are derived ONLY from these content-free events; we do NOT inspect user /data (e.g. wiki page counts) for engagement. This keeps the metrics pipeline cleanly SEPARATE from the abuse-audit pipeline (E8: content, 30d, break-glass, different purpose + access path). Enables offline retention / activation / app-popularity / 3rd-party-spawn-share analysis without touching user data. Emitter = CP (it owns session lifecycle). Affects E8 (separation note), CP/E1.
notes: Experiment design needs turn_count + session_end added to the event schema (session-level metadata counters, NOT content) for activation + retention metrics. See demo-experiment-design.md §2/§7.

title: DEMO-ROAST: GitHub-only storage inverts the target market
description: Demo storage = GitHub, but the pitch is NON-TECHNICAL users who don't have GitHub accounts and won't authorize an App grant. So the only people who can onboard are developers -- the segment LEAST impressed by a hosted wiki (they self-host). The demo is hardest for the intended user and least compelling for the reachable one. FIX: ship MANAGED storage (Spawnery-hosted, open-format, exportable) as the DEFAULT, with 'connect your own GitHub' as the power-user option (architecture already supports managed storage). Default any spawned/managed repo to PRIVATE (no public-repo abuse as free CDN). Affects E3, E6 onboarding, demo-mvp-scope, system §5.
notes: DECIDED: managed storage = DEFAULT (web-browsable + download/clone); Drive/iCloud/OneDrive (git bundle) = BYO-cloud option; GitHub = power-user. Spawned repos default private. Scope doc §4 updated.

title: DEMO-ROAST: demo is a weak experiment — doesn't test the thesis; no success metric
description: The business bet is differentiation (your-data + your-model + creator marketplace). The demo has no model choice (DeepSeek only), undercut data ownership (CP holds GitHub token, all audited), and ZERO marketplace (4 first-party apps, no creators). So a demo win only proves 'free hosted AI wiki is mildly appealing' (already suggested by NotebookLM/Mem/GPT-Store) and a loss is ambiguous (idea vs gutted execution). Also no defined success metric/audience/threshold => the experiment can't fail, so it can't inform go/no-go. And no retention hypothesis (why open the wiki on day 8?). FIX: define the explicit demo hypothesis + metric + audience before build; add a retention hook (e.g. weekly 'capture what you learned' nudge); be honest this validates one app's stickiness, not the platform.
notes:
ELABORATION (3 layers):
1) THESIS COVERAGE: tests ~1 of 5 load-bearing bets. H1 (wiki demand) weakly/confounded; H2 (data+model differentiation) NO; H3 (creators publish) NO; H4 (marketplace flywheel) NO; H5 (non-technical onboarding) anti-tested (GitHub gate filters them out).
2) CONFOUNDING: demo changes 5 vars at once vs ideal product (weak model, cold-start latency, GitHub wall, capped access, unrepresentative audience) => low retention is unattributable (idea? model? latency? onboarding? audience?). Unreadable signal.
3) NO METRIC/THRESHOLD/KILL-CRITERION: viral-tweet acquisition => will measure signups (vanity; measures tweet reach not product value). No pre-committed 'if X<Y by Z we do NOT build' => un-failable => can't inform go/no-go.
KEY RISK: the WIKI may retain WORST (PKM cold-start-value death spiral: only valuable once big, won't invest before valuable). The thing that breaks the spiral = proactive agent synthesis = the real differentiator = NOT in demo scope. So demo may ship the non-magical wiki and wrongly conclude 'wikis don't retain'. Irony: the coaches (cut by sp-jg3) have STRONGER built-in return loops (habit=daily, interview=event-driven) than the flagship.
META: demo conflates (a) technical proof [vertical slice does this, low risk] with (b) market validation [needs experiment design]. Don't read (a)'s usage as (b).
CONSTRUCTIVE: (i) name the single riskiest assumption (likely: does a personal LLM wiki retain a representative user past W2); (ii) test it CONCIERGE-style with 10-20 target non-technical users + high-quality model + manual glue BEFORE/around the build (~0 eng, removes confounders); (iii) pre-commit metric (activation + W2/W4 retention, NOT signups) + threshold + audience + decision; (iv) if building B+Y anyway, kill cheap confounders (managed storage sp-0a2, model-quality floor, proactive-synthesis if retention is what you measure).
UPDATE: open marketplace now IN demo scope -> the demo tests a slice of H3/H4 (do creators publish? do users spawn 3rd-party apps?). Improves thesis coverage from ~1/5 to ~2.5/5. STILL UNSOLVED: no success metric/threshold/representative-audience, and the wiki PKM cold-start-value retention risk. Define the experiment before build.
REFRAMED (user): the LLM Wiki is NOT the main bet -- the ARCHITECTURE across a portfolio of personal-agent ideas is. So the riskiest assumption is portfolio-level ('is this arch broadly effective + which ideas retain'), not wiki-specific. New epic E11 (sp-4jg) brainstorms candidate ideas + retention hypotheses, gating the seed lineup. Metrics are SESSION-EVENT-BASED only (sp-yjw, hourly Parquet, no /data inspection) -- the earlier content-based activation metric (>=3 wiki entries) is DROPPED. Concierge retention test (B1) still applies, to a few candidate ideas not just the wiki. Still open: pre-committed thresholds + representative audience.
E11 OUTPUT: seed lineup = Wiki+Research / Language Partner / Interview Coach / zork. Retention is now a PER-APP hypothesis read from content-free session events (sp-yjw): wiki = weakest loop, WATCH closely; language = the daily-loop bet; interview = episodic utility. The experiment = which idea the architecture serves best (portfolio), not 'does the wiki retain'. Still open: pre-committed thresholds + representative audience + the concierge pre-build retention test.
DRAFTED: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-spawnery-demo-experiment-design.md. Hypothesis (portfolio W2 retention, reactive) + content-free metrics (headline = W2 return, target cohort) + representative cohorts (A/B/C; early-adopter Z down-weighted) + concierge pre-build test (sp-cnc) + pre-committed decision table (proposed thresholds, team to ratify) + the proactivity branch (weak-reactive-retention -> build sp-37x + re-test, not kill). OPEN for the team: ratify thresholds + portfolio date.

title: DEMO-ROAST: pod resource limits + per-user quotas + isolation hardening
description: Single GPU box + plain containers + free tier = compute-abuse + free-inference-farming + DoS. One abuser can exhaust the GPU and deny the whole free tier (capacity ceiling = attack surface). FIX: cgroup CPU/mem/disk/pids limits on every pod (stop mining/fork-bombs/disk-fill); per-user concurrent-spawn cap; per-user daily token/inference quota; reconsider gVisor for the demo (cheap-ish, given the agent runs untrusted-user-steered code). Sybil friction: prefer GitHub-auth (more friction/age signal than throwaway Gmail) or a phone/age gate before free inference. Affects E1, E8 §3 free-tier signals.

title: ROAST: GitHub polling + per-turn persist = rate limits & I/O cliff
description: Found by ops #6/#7. (a) CP polls GitHub for latest tag->SHA at EVERY createSpawn + auto-resume: adds a sync round-trip, ~5000/hr installation rate-limit shared with provisioning+clone+push, and a hard dependency (GitHub down => no new auto spawns). (b) Persist per turn = git push every turn (rate-limit pressure) and for blob, 'git bundle --all' + FULL re-upload of entire history every turn => latency/bandwidth grow linearly with repo size (100MB+ per turn for an old spawn). FIX: cache resolved tag->SHA w/ TTL (periodic poll populates it; createSpawn never blocks on GitHub; fallback to last-known-good when GitHub down); debounce/coalesce persistence + background push; incremental blob bundles (since last ref) or pull folder-as-git-remote into MVP for blob. Affects E5 §2, E3 §2/§5.

Also no epic OWNS egress enforcement when it lands, and BYO-direct inference means the allowlist is per-spawn-dynamic (depends on the BYO baseUrl), not static 'provider+storage only'. FIX: align system §9 to post-MVP; assign egress-enforcement ownership to E1 (pod netns layer), configured from the manifest permission block + the spawn's model baseUrl; note the dynamic-allowlist complication. Affects system §9, E0 §9, E1.
notes: REOPENED + UN-DEFERRED: open third-party marketplace requires the manifest permissions block + spawn-time consent + egress ENFORCEMENT in the demo. Was incorrectly deferred when scope was first-party-only.

Also 'carry your repo to ANY Spawnery and re-spawn identically' — there is only ONE CP (self-hosted CP post-MVP), and spawn.yml references a CREATOR's definition repo you don't control (delist/delete => can't reconstruct). FIX: rewrite system §1 open-core constraint (self-host = node attaching to central CP; standalone post-MVP); split portability claim into 'content fully portable (plain files)' vs 'spawn config runs on Spawnery'; let users snapshot the App definition into their own repo so a delisted app still re-spawns. title: ROAST: 'one-click' is a lie — onboarding gauntlet for non-technical user
description: Found by product F7/F8/F13. First-run for the target user: OAuth + invent a vault passphrase + store a 24-word BIP-39 mnemonic + approve a GitHub per-repo grant + a 4-field wizard (App version / choose agent / choose model / choose storage) + cold-start wait. ~6-7 decision points, two of them (passphrase, seed phrase) alien concepts, for a user who wanted 'make a wiki'. 'Choose agent' is meaningless to them. FIX: genuine one-click default (managed storage + managed model + recommended agent); LAZY vault (no passphrase until first BYO key — free tier never needs it); hide agent choice behind Advanced; if a passphrase is set, make recovery codes mandatory not opt-in. Affects E6 §3, E4 §1/§3/§5, system §1.

title: ROAST: coding-agent-as-coach is unvalidated and load-bearing
description: Found by product F10 (arch #5). E7 §6 itself flags that a coding-style agent is tuned to 'do a file task,' not conversational coaching — then punts. 3 of 4 launch apps (habit/goal worst, sysdesign best, wiki fine) coerce a task-executor into a conversationalist via persona alone. A habit coach that answers 'I'm struggling' by grepping checkins/ and committing a file is uncanny — and it's the app that defines the emotional brand. FIX: dogfood the habit coach with real non-technical users for ~2 weeks; measure 'felt like a coach vs a robot'; be ready to drop habit coach from launch. Don't ship the weakest-fit app to prove generality. Affects E7 §1/§4/§6.
notes:
Doc gate strengthened in E7 §6 (dogfood habit coach ~2wk + free-DeepSeek compat check). Issue stays OPEN as real pre-launch validation work, not closable by docs.
E11 RESOLUTION: habit/goal coach DROPPED from the seed lineup (worst uncanny fit; needed proactive nudges, deferred). Seed coaches = Language Partner (tutor/DRILL role -> task-shaped, dodges the uncanny-warm-coach risk) + Interview Coach (structured task). The sp-pgw risk is largely sidestepped by choosing task-shaped roles over warm-coaching. Remaining validation: language tutoring quality on DeepSeek-Flash (sp-iui).

title: ROAST: vault hardening — recovery codes, KDF, Argon2 params
description: Found by security #4/#5 (product F13). (a) Multi-use recovery codes stored as wrap_rc at the CP = standing, non-expiring, undetectable skeleton key; CP compromise + the code = all BYO secrets forever, silently. (b) KDF(RC) is unspecified (only the passphrase path specifies Argon2id). (c) Argon2id 64MiB/t=3 tuned for browser perf is weak for operator-held ciphertext; no passphrase strength policy anywhere. (d) Recovery codes opt-in => non-technical user silently bricks vault. FIX: single-use recovery codes (invalidate wrap_rc on use); specify KDF(RC) (Argon2id or HKDF w/ domain separation); enforce passphrase strength floor + raise memory cost w/ progress UI; notify on any wrap_rc fetch; consider offline-only recovery (never upload wrap_rc). Affects E4 §3/§3a/§4/§8.

title: Visible spawn 'starting' lifecycle: async CreateSpawn + yellow dot, WS gated on active, 'waiting' header state
description: Today CP CreateSpawn blocks until the spawn is ACTIVE, so the web never shows a 'starting' period. Make CreateSpawn async: validate sync, store.Create(starting), return id immediately, provision in a background goroutine that SetActive/SetError on the node signal (status already DB-recorded). Web: createSpawn returns starting -> add to list (yellow dot) + setActiveId + conn=waiting, do NOT openSession; poll ListSpawns (1s while any starting, else 3s) and openSession when the active spawn flips to active (green), errored() on error (red, stays in sidebar, removable via Stop). useConnStatus/ConnStatus gain a 'waiting' state (grey pulse). Sidebar starting dot = yellow. Chat input disabled until connected. CP tests: createActive + suspend/resume tests wait-for-active after async CreateSpawn (new waitActive helper). spawnctl uses standalone spawn.v1, unaffected. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-02-spawn-starting-lifecycle-design.md.

title: Node-relay transcript recording + replay (history survives browser reload, both lanes)
description: The slice-2 spawn/history replay lives in the in-pod acpadapter, which is only in the CRI/runsc lane — so in the Docker/self-hosted lane (just dev) history is lost on browser reload (client buffer gone, no adapter in path). Move recording to the NODE relay (in both lanes path, outlives client reloads): extract the recorder to internal/transcript (shared with the adapter); node holds a long-lived per-spawn recorder registry, tees relay bytes, and replays spawn/history on each client (re)connect. Lane-aware (node records Docker lane only; CRI keeps the adapter) to avoid double-replay. Web onHistory/historyToItems unchanged. Goal: history survives reload in just dev, proven by an e2e test. Follows-on from sp-r6b/sp-02f; partial step toward sp-3nb (durable/cross-device history).
close_reason: Node-relay transcript recording + replay. Recorder extracted to internal/transcript (shared w/ adapter); node records each spawn's transcript at the relay + replays spawn/history on every client (re)connect, Docker lane only (lane-aware). History now survives a browser reload in just dev — proven by e2e (10/10, reload test wipes the client buffer so only node replay can restore). 3 tasks two-stage reviewed; final review READY-TO-MERGE.

title: Node should retry/reconnect to CP (not exit when CP is down)
description: Observed during just dev: if CP isn't up yet at node startup (or the CP drops), node.Run returns an error and cmd/spawnlet log.Fatals -> the node exits immediately. Fix: a reconnect loop with backoff so the node waits for the CP at startup and reconnects if the stream drops (re-Register; the CP already reconciles a returning node per the state-machine design). Only exit on ctx cancellation (clean shutdown). The Manager + its running spawns persist across reconnects (mgr is passed into node.Run; a fresh attacher re-registers).
close_reason: DONE merged a8343cd. node.Run refactored into a reconnect loop + per-connection runOnce: dials+Registers+heartbeats+receives under a per-connection connCtx (defer cancel stops heartbeat + relay sessions on disconnect, no goroutine leak); backoff 1s->30s exp (reset after >30s healthy); returns ONLY on ctx cancellation. cmd/spawnlet log.Fatal(node.Run) with ctx=Background now loops forever (reconnects) instead of dying when CP is down. Fixes the just-dev startup race + CP-restart resilience. mgr persists across reconnects (running spawns survive). Hermetic TestRunRetriesUntilContextCancelled (unreachable CP -> returns context err after retrying, not the conn err). First internal/node test (partial sp-sj8).

title: [epic] Adopt gVisor (runsc) for the cloud tier — Phase 2 isolation
description: Research verdict (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-gvisor-isolation-research-results.md): adopt gVisor on the cloud tier now, on top of the existing floor. Same isolation as Cloud Run/GKE Sandbox/Modal/Tencent for ~identical workload; closes the host-kernel + runc-escape CVE class (CVE-2019-5736, CVE-2024-21626, CVE-2020-14386, CVE-2022-0185) that runc+seccomp does not. Plumbing exists (CONTAINER_RUNTIME=runsc knob). Plan: PHASE 1 (pre-req, cheap) read-only rootfs + drop-all caps + user-namespace remap + MOVE egress floor to host veth (see floor-placement bug). PHASE 2 install runsc (systrap platform, directfs+overlay+netstack defaults) + canary cloud spawns 5%->25%->100% + per-image runc fallback w/ audit + CI compat gate. cgroup limits (sp-ach) STAY (gVisor doesn't self-enforce in-sandbox limits — pin sandbox to host cgroup). PHASE 3 (threshold-gated) microVM (Kata/Cloud Hypervisor) for a hard tier: regulated/enterprise/high-value tenants, GPU/eBPF/FUSE/io_uring needs, or /dev/kvm available. Run our own benchmark vs real agent traffic before sizing.
notes: BLOCKER found in local goose+sidecar runsc verification (sp-vaw, P0): the current Docker two-container shared-netns pod (agent --network=container:<sidecar>, reaches sidecar on 127.0.0.1:8080) is BROKEN under per-container gVisor — each container is its own Sentry/netstack, loopback not shared. Verified: goose ITSELF is gVisor-compatible (boots+ACP+prompt under runsc); host egress floor (sp-ff2), cap-drop (sp-s9u), and runsc preflight (sp-zb2) all work under runsc on the real pod. The ONLY break is inter-container loopback. So Phase-2 needs a pod-as-one-sandbox model (containerd CRI pod / k8s RuntimeClass) OR collapsing sidecar+agent into one container — NOT raw docker-run-per-container. Decide in sp-vaw before any runsc cloud rollout.

title: sp-pc4 part 3: persist container node_id + compensating cleanup on post-Provision failure
description: From the part-2 CP-rewire review. Three related robustness gaps deferred to part 3 (the inventory/reconciliation protocol): (1) CreateSpawn orphan window — if SetActive fails AFTER Provision succeeded (route bound + node container live), CreateSpawn returns CodeInternal with NO cleanup; the container + route leak and the row is stuck 'starting' (boot sweep flips it unreachable but the container/route survive). Add compensating rt.Drop + StopOnNode + SetError on the post-Provision failure path. (2) SetError's error is swallowed (_ =) on Provision failure — at least log it so a stuck 'starting' row isn't silent. (3) The container's node_id is NEVER persisted: Create inserts NodeID='' and SetActive only flips phase; Provision returns the chosen node id but CreateSpawn discards it. So spawn_containers.node_id stays empty and LiveContainersByNode/Adopt (the part-3 reconcile machinery) cannot function until the CP persists the node binding (e.g. SetActive should also set the container's node_id, or add a bind step). All three are the same theme: record the node binding + run compensating cleanup on the post-Provision edge. Part of sp-pc4 part 3 (lifecycle RPCs + generation fencing + inventory reconciliation).
close_reason: Done in sp-pc4 part 3a (merged 0978c24): node_id persisted via SetActive + compensating cleanup on the post-Provision orphan window. (Was filed depending-on sp-pc4, but it's a completed sub-item; sp-pc4 stays open for part 3b.)

description: Replace the CP's ephemeral in-memory maps with a durable transactional state layer (Bun + modernc sqlite + Postgres + goose + :memory: tests), and implement the ENTIRE CP-side spawn lifecycle on it. spawns table = the CP index. Owns: store package (schema owners/apps/app_versions/spawns/spawn_mounts, full interface, Bun impl, hermetic tests); CP-side state machine (starting/active/suspending/suspended/error/deleted) incl. transitions, boot + node-evict reconciliation (->suspended), idempotent seeding; lifecycle RPCs CreateSpawn(rewired)/ListSpawns/SuspendSpawn/ResumeSpawn/DeleteSpawn + Session ownership-via-store + auto-resume-on-attach + single-session takeover; CP->node plumbing (new Suspend message; StartSpawn carries mount bindings). Node-side persist/restore is a STUB teardown here (data survives only once E3 persistent storage + sp-gd9 WIP-ref land). DB authoritative for ownership; router.Owner() removed. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-state-dao-layer-design.md.
design: Hard constraints: ergonomic cross-repo transactions; thin-ORM scanning (no manual row->struct); SQL stays visible (no query-hiding ORM magic); one codebase over two dialects; strong (not absolute) pref for pure-Go/cgo-free; mature/boring, no bleeding edge. SQLite/Postgres are SEED options only — research must challenge the engine choice.

title: Orphaned-container handling: labels + reap-on-startup + idle reaper
description: The spawnlet's SpawnStore is in-memory, so on spawnlet restart it forgets running pods while Docker keeps them alive -> orphans. Also no idle-timeout in the slice, so clients that never call StopSpawn leak pods. DEFERRED from the slice (user decision: track only). Plan when picked up: (1) label every container at create (spawnery.managed=true, spawnery.spawn-id=<id>); (2) reconcile-on-startup: list labeled containers and remove orphans (clean slate; in-mem store is empty post-restart); (3) background IDLE-REAPER goroutine that tears down spawns idle past a timeout -- this is the E1 idle-timeout feature and is the only part needing an always-running process. Pairs with E1 runtime core (sp-ei4). For now: manual StopSpawn + docker rm for stragglers during dev.
notes: Web Playwright e2e (sp-8mn) reproduces the leak: globalTeardown SIGTERMs the spawnlet but its stub+sidecar containers keep running (one stub + one sidecar per run). Graceful container-teardown on spawnlet SIGTERM would prevent it outright (complements reconcile-on-startup). e2e cleans up via 'docker rm -f --filter ancestor' for now.

It is simultaneously several apps' reason-to-be (CRM follow-ups, wiki proactive cross-linking, language spaced-repetition) AND the portfolio's retention ENGINE (a proactive nudge = a return-loop driver; direct antidote to sp-54f). title: ROAST: cold-start-every-wake = bad UX for coaches; no latency budget
description: Found by 3/4 (arch #7, product F9, ops #5). Realistic first-token on resume = 5-15s warm-image/small-repo, 30s-2min+ cold-image/large-repo/busy-GPU (image pull + pod create + git clone /data + agent boot + skill load + key agreement + model first token). Coaches' whole value is continuity, but every 'checking in' pays full cold-start + full-thread reload — and the loyal daily user with a big thread gets the WORST latency + re-billed replay tokens. No doc owns this number. FIX: pull warm-pool/keep-alive + node repo-cache (resume=fetch not clone) + pre-pulled images + incremental/summarized thread load into MVP for coaching apps; loading-state UX in E6; set a first-token-on-resume acceptance metric. Affects E1 §3/§4, E6 §3, E7.
notes: RESOLVED (user): accept cold-start startup times for MVP; warm-pool/repo-cache/incremental-load DECLINED. Key correction: cold start = NEW session, NO conversation-thread replay. Continuity = agent reads structured files in /data at session start (not transcript-into-context). Kills the 'loyal user worst latency' + 're-bill replay' concerns. Tune idle timeout to match LLM provider prompt-cache TTL (~5min) since cache expires regardless. DOC FIX NEEDED: E1 §3 / E7 §4 / E6 §3 currently say resume 'reloads the prior thread to restore context' -- correct to 'fresh session; continuity via file reads in /data'.
close_reason: Resolved: accept cold-start times; continuity = file reads not thread replay (demo-mvp-scope §4)

description: Permission/consent enforcement; egress allowlist enforcement; audit pipeline (sidecar conversation-content logging for Spawnery-operated cloud only); abuse signals; flagging/takedown for the open registry. Design doc section 9.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e8-trust-safety-audit-design.md. Decisions: audit = full content on Spawnery-operated infra, encrypted, ~30d TTL auto-purge, break-glass access, disclosed (self-host exempt, BYO-on-cloud audited); review = scanner-first (LLM-as-judge over manifest+persona+skills) human-on-flag; enforcement = tiered (live block severe-only + async delist/suspend leaving user data untouched + appeal); inline classifier shares infra with review scanner (real ML build item); free-tier content-blind abuse signals. E8 is the MVP safety net standing in for deferred permission/egress enforcement.

title: Make ResumeSpawn async like CreateSpawn (currently blocks the RPC during Provision)
description: sp-wi3 made CreateSpawn async (return + provision in background). ResumeSpawn (internal/cp/lifecycle.go) still runs scheduler.Provision inline holding the per-spawn lock for the node's startup latency (seconds), blocking the client RPC. For consistency + the same 'starting' UX on resume, make ResumeSpawn async too (suspended->starting->background provision->active/error). Flagged in sp-wi3 Task A review.

title: UI: WS connection status indicator (text + colored status light) in chat header
description: Replace the chat header status element (web/src/shell/AppShell.tsx: ready) with a WebSocket connection-status indicator: a status light (colored dot) + text reflecting the live ws state. States: GRAY 'connecting' while the ws is connecting; GREEN 'connected' once open/ready; YELLOW once connecting has exceeded a timeout (~5s, configurable) without connecting; RED 'error' when the ws errored. Source the state from App.tsx's ws lifecycle (openSession onopen/onerror/onclose + a 5s timer); thread a structured status (state enum + label) down to the header instead of the current free-text 'status' string. Keep data-testid="status" (and add data-status for the light/e2e). Update the e2e/unit selectors that assert status text 'ready' (chat.spec waits toHaveText('ready'); the marketplace spec too) to the new 'connected' text or a data-status check. Hermetic vitest for the indicator states + a timer test; e2e still green.
close_reason: WS status indicator shipped: useConnStatus hook (state machine + 5s slow timer) + ConnStatus component (gray-pulse connecting… / amber-pulse slow / green connected / red error, hidden when no live socket); wired into App/AppShell replacing the free-text status. Vitest (hook+component, incl. fake-timer slow + error-preserved-on-close) + tsc clean; e2e 10/10 (selectors -> 'connected'). 2 tasks two-stage reviewed. Merged to master.

title: MarkBootUnreachable doesn't sweep 'suspending' — CP crash mid-suspend strands the spawn
description: In SuspendSpawn (internal/cp/lifecycle.go), if the CP crashes after SetSuspending but before SetSuspended, the spawn is stranded in 'suspending': the in-handler SetError compensation is bypassed, and store.MarkBootUnreachable only sweeps {Starting, Active}, so boot reconcile won't recover it. Narrow window (a couple synchronous store calls; container already torn down). Fix: add 'suspending' to MarkBootUnreachable's status set (or a dedicated boot sweep that errors/recreates stranded suspending spawns). Found in sp-zer final whole-branch review. Epic sp-k20.

title: Update web e2e chat.spec.ts to the Marketplace-default / nav-spawn flow
description: sp-wsv removed the static Chat tab + mount-time auto-spawn. web/e2e/chat.spec.ts (host-gated Playwright, excluded from npm test/vitest) still clicks getByTestId('nav-chat') and assumes chat-as-default auto-spawn — now stale. Update it to the new flow: default Marketplace, spawn an app (e.g. Secret App / spawnery/secret-app) from Browse, then click nav-spawn to open its chat. Run via npm run test:e2e on a host with browsers + the stack.
close_reason: DONE merged d16fad4. Updated web/e2e: chat.spec spawns 'Secret App' (spawnery/secret-app) from the Marketplace then chats (nav-spawn to return), marketplace.spec drops the pre-spawn status=ready waits (no auto-spawn; market is default), global-setup runs the e2e node self-hosted+EGRESS_ENFORCE=false (root-free, matches sp-11p). All 4 Playwright tests PASS (real Chromium + cp+node+vite + stubagent/sidecar containers, ~11s).

title: DockerPodBackend.StartPod: tolerate empty pod IP when floor not enforced
description: StartPod now fetches ContainerIP unconditionally and errors if empty; on master ContainerIP was fetched only when egressEnforced. Regression risk (low — our bridge sidecar always has an IP): a non-enforcing self-hosted spawn (NODE_CLASS=self-hosted + EGRESS_ENFORCE=false) whose sidecar has no bridge IP would now fail StartPod where master spawned fine. Fix options: make StartPod tolerate an empty PodIP (return handle with empty IP) + have Manager fail-closed only when egressEnforced && PodIP=='' (preserves both no-regression AND floor safety). Revisit before/with the CRI slice sp-d3t (which reworks pod IP via PodSandboxStatus). Found in sp-0wd final review.
close_reason: SUBSUMED + DONE in sp-11p (merged 80d1767): DockerPodBackend.StartPod now best-effort on ContainerIP/PID; Manager.Create fail-closes only when egressEnforced()&&PodIP==''. Required for rootless Podman (no bridge IP). Tests: TestCreateEmptyIP{FailsClosedWhenEnforced,SucceedsWhenNotEnforced} + TestDockerPodBackendStartPodToleratesMissingIPAndPID.

title: internal/node: add relay test coverage
description: internal/node has ZERO test files — this is what let the sp-7wa critical gap (CP-attached openSession still using Docker Attach) ship green through per-task review; only the final whole-branch review caught it. Add a hermetic test harness for the attacher (fake CP bidi stream + FakeRuntime + injectable attach) covering startSpawn/openSession/feed/closeSession, so the CP-attached relay path is exercised. Found in sp-7wa final review.

title: AttachACP: non-linux build stub
description: runtime.AttachACP lives in attach_acp_linux.go (linux-only) but server.go/node/attach.go reference it unconditionally, so GOOS=darwin go build ./internal/spawnlet fails (undefined: runtime.AttachACP). Node stack is linux-only by design + no CI cross-builds, so non-blocking. Add attach_acp_other.go (//go:build !linux) returning an 'unsupported on this OS' error so cross-platform builds compile. Found in sp-7wa final review.

title: web: version selector on app Detail to spawn a specific (unverified) version
description: sp-t5p lets an author spawn an unverified version on their self-hosted node, but the web Detail 'Spawn' button sends no version -> CreateSpawn resolves LatestReviewed. Add a version picker on Detail (versions already shown) so onSpawn can pass a chosen version; author can then spawn their unverified version from the UI. CreateSpawnRequest.version already supported (slice 3).

(2) cmd/spawnlet getenvBool only matches 1/true/TRUE; use strings.EqualFold for false/False/yes consistency (cosmetic; cloud path ignores the knob). (3) Optional: comment that the nsenter applier assumes one-netns-per-spawn (no chain flush; safe today).

title: store: test the 0002 trust-tier migration backfill (reviewed=true -> tier='reviewed')
description: Spec 2026-06-01-e5-catalog-read-surface-slice1.md §7 lists a migration-backfill test that was not implemented: NewTestStore runs 0001+0002 on an empty DB so the backfill UPDATE always hits zero rows. Add a test that opens the store at migration 0001 (goose UpTo), inserts an app_version with reviewed=1, migrates to 0002, and asserts tier='reviewed'. Low risk (no prod data) but closes the coverage gap. Backfill SQL itself is correct by inspection.

title: E5: spawnctl default -app-id 'secret-app' no longer seeded by CP
description: cmd/cp/main.go now seeds spawnery/{wiki,language,interview,zork} (sp-0sc), not 'secret-app'. cmd/spawnctl/main.go:30 still defaults -app-id to 'secret-app', so a manual spawnctl spawn against a fresh prod DB fails to resolve the app. Tests unaffected (self-seed). Fix when slice 2 (registration + real definition repos) lands: set spawnctl default to 'spawnery/zork' (the smoke-test app) or whatever the real demo definition is. Out of scope for the read-surface slice.

title: EPIC: tmux attach to a running spawn (raw terminal access)
description: Let a user/operator attach an interactive terminal (tmux) session into a RUNNING spawn's container, separate from the ACP chat channel — for debugging, inspection, and power-user workflows. Only valid on an 'active' spawn (a live container episode exists); attaching to a suspended spawn would auto-resume first (per lifecycle). Path: client -> CP -> node -> exec into the spawn's agent container (tmux/PTY), relayed as opaque bytes through the existing CP transparent-relay (like ACP, a distinct stream/channel). Concerns to design: it's a second concurrent attachment to the container (interacts with the single-ACP-session + single-writer storage assumptions — terminal access can mutate /app files out-of-band, so it must be reflected in suspend's dirty-tree capture and counts as activity for the idle timer); permissioning (owner-only; on Spawnery-operated/audited nodes a raw shell bypasses the sidecar audit point — trust/safety implications, coordinate with E8); resource/teardown (a tmux session keeping a spawn alive past idle). Likely post-MVP. Depends on the spawn lifecycle + running-container entity (sp-gd9) and the node runtime (E1).
design: Open question: PTY-exec into the existing agent container vs a sidecar 'shell' container sharing the netns/mounts. Activity from the tmux session must reset the idle timer (node-side, already byte-observable). Audit: a raw shell is an un-audited mutation path on operated nodes — gate behind trust tier / disclosure.

title: Conversation continuity across suspend/resume
description: Deferred from the spawn-lifecycle design (data-only resume for now). Persist the conversation transcript to the data repo per spawn.yml's conversation-history pointer on suspend; reload/replay on resume so a resumed spawn continues the prior chat. Gated per-app (some apps are stateless-per-session). Builds on the spawn lifecycle (sp); see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-spawn-lifecycle-design.md sec 5/10.

title: POST-MVP EPIC: Native spawn-to-spawn comms framework
description:
Enabling substrate for multi-spawn orchestration. Today a spawn drives ONE agent over ACP via the CP; there is no native primitive for an app/spawn to message or create sibling/child spawns ('app-spawns-app'). This epic adds a native inter-spawn communication + orchestration primitive: an orchestrator spawn that can create and drive child spawns, and/or spawn<->spawn messaging, built on the existing CP router/scheduler + transparent ACP relay.

WHY: prerequisite for native eval orchestration, sub-agent spawning, and agent-team orchestration. It is the 'communication engine' those tools build on.

SECURITY CRUX (hard requirement): spawn-spawning is sensitive under the open-marketplace trust model — open third-party apps must NOT get unscoped spawn-creation. The capability must be permissioned via the manifest permissions/consent/egress model and trust tiers (a scanner miss must stay contained). Quotas/concurrency caps from sp-ach apply.

SCOPE NOTE: post-MVP (after the demo's safety floor + marketplace). The demo's spawn engine (CP mediation slice, merged) is the foundation. Capture only; design later via brainstorming.

title: POST-MVP EPIC: Native eval orchestration (one spawn evals/optimizes another)
description:
Eval-first LLM-app-building tooling, implemented as multi-spawn orchestration on the native comms framework. Roles, each a spawn/agent over the same communication engine: SUBJECT (app under test, possibly weaker/cheaper model) + DRIVER/user-simulator (feeds inputs / a fixed eval set) + JUDGE/OPTIMIZER (stronger model). Loop: run subject on the eval set -> judge scores against a rubric -> optimizer proposes prompt/instruction edits -> re-eval -> keep if better; promote best app@sha and/or per-model prompt.

WHY IT FITS: (1) makes the 'model half' thesis concrete — per-app, per-model scores answer 'which AI is good enough for which app', and the optimizer can auto-tune an app's prompt per model. (2) Marketplace CAPABILITY signal complementing the safety trust-tiers (e.g. 'scores 8.2/10 with DeepSeek') — could become CI-for-prompts in the publish flow. (3) Dogfoods multi-spawn orchestration. (4) Immediate internal use: discharges 'verify seed apps run on DeepSeek' (sp-iui).

START NARROW: measurement-only harness first (run app over eval set vs a model, judge-score vs rubric, report per-model scores; reuse internal/acp like spawnctl) — NO optimizer. Add the optimize loop as phase 2 once the eval signal is trusted.

DESIGN CRUXES: (a) eval dataset/rubric is the real artifact — creator-authored; shapes the manifest. (b) LLM-as-judge is noisy/biased/gameable — needs rubric + held-out set + mitigations (reference-guided, pairwise, juries) to avoid reward-hacking the judge. (c) keep optimization surface tiny: {prompt variants} x {models} before tools/data. (d) cost & nondeterminism -> offline/power-creator feature, competes with live users on the single demo box. (e) app-or-infra: prototype as an app/external harness (needs the spawn-comms capability), graduate useful parts into publish/CI.

PRIOR ART (survey before building): DSPy (MIPROv2/Bootstrap), OPRO, APE, EvoPrompt, TextGrad for optimization; promptfoo/Braintrust/LangSmith/Inspect/OpenAI-Evals for harnesses; LLM-as-judge bias literature. A deep-research prompt was drafted (2026-05-31 session) to map SOTA + gap analysis. Novel angle = eval/optimize loop as native multi-spawn orchestration on an isolated, model-agnostic, versioned-app substrate feeding a marketplace.

DEPENDS ON: the native spawn-to-spawn comms framework epic. Capture only; design later via brainstorming.

title: Web: load Inter/JetBrains Mono fonts (declared in globals.css but never loaded)
description: globals.css declares --font-sans: Inter and --font-mono: JetBrains Mono, but no @font-face / <link> / @fontsource is present, so both silently fall back to ui-sans-serif/ui-monospace. Either add the font loading (preconnect + stylesheet or @fontsource deps) or drop the declarations. Surfaced by the final review of the web UI framework adoption branch. Minor/visual.

title: Web client deferrals (wss, permission shape, tool-chip update)
description: Minor follow-ups from the web ACP client final review (all documented/tracked, none blocking): (1) App.tsx hardcodes ws:// — use wss:// under HTTPS when production static-serving lands. (2) acp/client.ts session/request_permission response shape is TO-CONFIRM against the real ACP spec (kind-based option pick is a guess; secret-word demo doesn't trigger it) — confirm during a live permission-triggering run. (3) ui: onToolUpdate appends a new chip titled 'tool' instead of updating the original tool_call chip by toolCallId — use a Map<toolCallId,chip>. (4) WS OriginPatterns:['*'] dev-open — tighten at CP/auth (ties to sp-l5o authz review). Part of E6 (sp-95v).

title: Local-app data sync + ACP client (Obsidian/IDE) for spawns
description:
VISION: let users view/edit a spawn's data in a native local app (Obsidian for the wiki; an IDE for code) while the agent works it remotely, and chat with the agent from the same app via an ACP-client plugin. The killer combo is **llm-wiki + an Obsidian ACP-client plugin**: the vault shows the wiki growing in real time while the user chats in the same window.

TWO SYNC LEVELS:
- (start) READ-ONLY local mirror, continuously updated from the remote. Sidesteps bidirectional sync; edits flow through the agent (chat). For git-backed mounts this is nearly FREE (user clones their own data repo + a tiny daemon git-pulls on push). Trap: Obsidian IS an editor, so users will edit the mirror and silently lose changes on the next pull -> must be enforced read-only or upgraded to bidir.
- (target, RICHER) BIDIRECTIONAL real-time sync: user and agent collaborate LIVE on the same files -> the agent researches/writes the bulk while the user views/corrects, and both see each other's edits in real time. This is the powerful version. Hard part = real-time bidirectional sync + conflict resolution (CRDT/OT per-file e.g. Yjs for text, or fast file-granular last-write-wins). PRIOR ART: Obsidian's livesync community plugins (self-hosted CouchDB/CRDT) are a precedent for exactly this shape. Overlaps with E3's DEFERRED bidirectional sync (sp-u53).

TWO DEPLOYMENT MODES (from the ACP-client research):
- REMOTE: mirror/sync for display + ACP chat with TRIMMED fs capability (agent stays self-contained on container data); two channels (sync + ACP). A network/stdio bridge is required (all current ACP clients are stdio + local-assuming).
- SELF-HOST: mount the user's local folder straight into the spawn as the data mount, keep ACP fs capability INTACT, REWRITE PATHS (vault path <-> /app/data). No sync needed -> bidirectional by nature (same folder).

RELATED / STRATEGIC:
- We will build our OWN ACP client (existing ones are IDE-shaped: assume local checkout, want an IDE). Study Zed + VS Code ACP-client impls for the client-half rendering (session/update, tool-calls, permission prompts), drop the IDE scaffolding.
- This is arguably the answer to the wiki RETENTION problem (sp-54f) WITHOUT building proactivity (sp-37x): the wiki lives in a tool you already open daily. And it makes 'your data is yours' visceral.
- ACP-client research (network bridge required; fs trim for remote; local-mount+path-rewrite for self-host) was confirmed via deep research; bridge idea = spawnctl refactored into a transparent stdio<->Session pipe + minimal initialize capability handshake.

OUT OF SCOPE for now; parked as a future direction.
notes:
SYNC OPTIONS (a menu, none decided):
1) git pull/push loop — already have it; commit-granular, coarse.
2) CouchDB/PouchDB replication (Obsidian Self-hosted LiveSync shape) — OFF-THE-SHELF, near-real-time SAVE-granular w/ MVCC conflict-merge. Arch: CouchDB hub <-> user's Obsidian (reuse the existing LiveSync plugin) <-> a spawn-side /app/data<->CouchDB replicator (the only piece WE build); one DB per spawn. NOT a character-CRDT. Downside: needs a hosted CouchDB. = ONE OPTION.
3) CRDT keystroke-realtime (Yjs/Automerge) — true simultaneous editing, conflict-free auto-merge. DESIRED to explore eventually.

PREFERENCE (user): AVOID hosting an external DB just for sync. Favor PEER-TO-PEER: the user's local app and the spawn synchronize DIRECTLY. Fits us well — the existing client<->spawn channel (per-session E2E / direct / relay) could carry the sync updates, no central DB. Prior art for P2P: LiveSync has a P2P/WebRTC mode; Yjs (y-webrtc) + Automerge are natively P2P. IDEAL TARGET = CRDT replicas on both peers (local app + spawn's /app/data), synced over the client<->spawn channel, keystroke-realtime, no hosted DB. Note the data-substrate tension (git = durable/portable/clean-exit vs CRDT/live-store = realtime collab); likely git as source-of-truth + a realtime layer reconciling to it.

title: Mountpoint creation for data mounts (avoid per-app .gitkeep)
description: runc can't create the nested mount destination /app/<path> inside an already-read-only /app, so the mount dir must pre-exist in the bind source. Slice workaround: each app ships an empty mount dir (examples/secret-app/data/.gitkeep). Cleaner options: (a) node mkdirs appPath/<mt.Path> before the ro bind (host dir is writable; mutates the app source dir); (b) when /app is assembled per-spawn from App@sha (full design), assembly creates the mountpoints. Decide + implement when /app assembly lands. Found during the per-mount-backends e2e (mounting rw /app/data onto ro /app failed until data/ existed in the source).

title: spawnlet: image auto-pull in StartContainer/manager (spec §8 step 3)
description: runtime.Docker.StartContainer assumes the image exists locally; spec §8 step 3 said 'ensure agent base image present -- pull if missing'. Not implemented (images built locally per README). Add cli.ImagePull on not-found before create, or in Manager.Create. Final-review finding. Acceptable for the slice; needed once images come from a registry (CP integration).

title: spawnlet: thread SidecarPort to the agent's OPENAI_HOST (or drop the knob)
description: ManagerConfig.SidecarPort is configurable, but deploy/agent/entrypoint.sh + the Goose OPENAI_HOST hardcode 127.0.0.1:8080. If SidecarPort != 8080 the agent silently can't reach inference. Fix: either drop the SidecarPort knob (slice always uses 8080) or thread the port into the agent container's OPENAI_HOST env in Manager.Create. Final-review finding (whole-slice). Harmless today (nothing overrides default).

title: Cloud burst
description: DEFERRED (post-MVP). Autoscaler/provisioner; cloud node agents; burst inference + container routing; capacity signaling from the home machine. Design doc sections 4,12 (sub-project 9).

title: Billing, payments & rev-share
description: DEFERRED (post-MVP). Metering->billing; subscription tiers; BYOK tier; premium-models / private-apps gating; creator payouts and marketplace rev-share. Design doc sections 12,13 (sub-project 10).

title: Post-demo: durable/true spawn history (agent memory across reconnect + cross-device + survives suspend)
description: Beyond the visual adapter-replay: true agent memory on reconnect (ACP session/load if goose supports) and/or CP/store transcript persistence that survives suspend and works cross-device. Gated alongside E3 persistent storage. From epic sp-k20 spec §Post-demo.

title: Post-demo: on-demand history pagination (remove acpadapter 500-item cap)
description: Lazy/windowed transcript loading for long sessions; removes the slice-2 adapter 500-item cap + truncation marker. From epic sp-k20 spec §Post-demo.

title: web marketplace UI polish nits (sp-znq follow-ups)
description: Optional polish from sp-znq final review (all non-blocking): (1) App.tsx clientRef.current not nulled on re-spawn teardown (unreachable via UI since busy disables input; tidiness). (2) Detail.tsx renders createdAt as raw epoch string (int64 Connect-JSON) — format as a date. (3) Publish.tsx no client-side required-field validation (relies on CP InvalidArgument toast; a pre-submit guard saves a round-trip). (4) Publish mount rows keyed by array index (harmless given append/filter-only).

title: DeleteSpawn/StopSpawn ignore auth ok-bool (owner, _ :=) — return Unauthenticated for consistency
description: Minor consistency nit from the part-3a final review. DeleteSpawn + StopSpawn use 'owner, _ := auth.OwnerFromContext' (ignore the ok bool) and delegate ownership to stop()'s store check; ListSpawns/CreateSpawn check ok and return CodeUnauthenticated. Safe in prod (the auth.Interceptor rejects unauthenticated before the handler), but if stop() is ever called from a non-HTTP path the empty owner yields a misleading PermissionDenied. Low priority: align DeleteSpawn+StopSpawn to check ok -> Unauthenticated. Also (minor) add an explicit store-layer TestMarkDeletedFromStarting.
